zh.Lochbroom, Ross-shire, Scotland, United: 英國蘇格蘭洛-爾洛賀布盧姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail於1434年出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾登利時，她的父親John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn64歲，母親Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell44歲。她於大約1454年與Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie在蘇格蘭洛-爾金泰爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1503年7月8日在蘇格蘭洛-爾吉勒亞南享年69歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Lochbroom, Ross-shire, Scotland, United: 英国苏格兰洛-爾洛賀布盧姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail于1434年出生在苏格兰阿吉爾登利时，她的父亲John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn64岁，母亲Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell44岁。她于大约1454年与Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie在苏格兰洛-爾金泰爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1503年7月8日在苏格兰洛-爾吉勒亞南享年69岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: MacDougall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McDougall
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Dubhghaill, ‘son of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was originally a byname used to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from the fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDowell and Doyle .

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail was born in 1434, in Dunollie, Argyll, Scotland, her father, John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn, was 64 and her mother, Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell, was 44. She married Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie about 1454, in Kintail, Ross-shire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 8 July 1503, in Killearnan, Ross-shire, Scotland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail est née en 1434, à Dunollie, Argyll, Écosse, son père, John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn, avait 64 ans et sa mère, Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell, avait 44 ans. Elle a épousé Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie environ 1454, à Kintail, Ross-shire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 8 juillet 1503, à Killearnan, Ross-shire, Écosse, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail 1434 in Dunollie, Argyll, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn 64 und ihre Mutter Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell 44. Sie hat um 1454 in Kintail, Ross-shire, Scotland Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 8. Juli 1503 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Killearnan, Ross-shire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail è nata il nel 1434 nel Dunollie, Argyll, Scotland, sua padre, John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn, aveva 64 anni e sua madre, Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell, aveva 44 anni. Lei sposato Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie circa 1454, nel Kintail, Ross-shire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 8 luglio 1503, nel Killearnan, Ross-shire, Scotland, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintailが1434年にスコットランドアルグイルダノリーで生まれた時，父John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lornは64歳，母Gyllis "Egidia" Campbellは44歳であった。約1454年にスコットランドロス-シュアイアキンティイルでAlexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1503年7月8日にスコットランドロス-シュアイアキレアルナンで69歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail(이)가 1434년 스컷랜드 아그이 던리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn(은)는 64세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 대략 1454년 스컷랜드 로스-실르 킨테일에서 Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1503년 7월 8일 스컷랜드 로스-실르 킬리른아에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail nasceu em 1434, em Dunollie, Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn, tinha 64 anos e sua mãe, Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell, tinha 44 anos. Ela casou-se com Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie aproximadamente 1454, em Kintail, Ross-shire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 8 de julho de 1503, em Killearnan, Ross-shire, Scotland, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail родилась в 1434 г. в местности дунолли, аргилл, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn было 64 года, а матери, которую звали Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell, 44. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie около 1454 г. в местности кинтеил, росс-шир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 8 июля 1503 г. в местности киллирнан, росс-шир, Шотландия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anna Margaret MacDougall Baroness of Kintail nació en 1434, en Dunollie, Argyll, Scotland, su papá, John - Iain MacDougall 11th of Dunollie and Lorn, tenía 64 años y su mamá, Gyllis "Egidia" Campbell, tenía 44 años. Se casó con Alexander Mackenzie 6th of Kintail, 1st Chief of Clan Mackenzie aproximadamente 1454, en Kintail, Ross-shire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 8 de julio de 1503, en Killearnan, Ross-shire, Scotland, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
